title: Politics Truth Stack
summary: Treat politics as the study of power, institutions, legitimacy, incentives, and collective decision-making. The strongest starting posture is Australia first, then comparative systems second.
starterPack: Start with Parliament, the Constitution, PM&C guidance, the AEC, and the High Court before reading commentary.
Use committee reports, ANAO work, and official inquiries to understand what the system says about itself when things go wrong.
Read mature interpreters after you have anchored yourself in primary records.

tiers.title: First Principles
tiers.description: The durable ideas underneath political noise.

items.name: Politics allocates power and values
items.description: Politics is about who decides, who benefits, who pays, and what institutions make the decision stick.

items.name: Legitimacy matters
items.description: Power lasts better when people accept it as lawful, fair, and procedurally valid.

items.name: Every system trades speed, freedom, accountability, and control
items.description: Regimes and institutions optimise different things and pay different costs.

tiers.title: Primary Authorities
tiers.description: The closest formal records of Australian political reality.

items.name: Parliament of Australia
items.description: Bills, committees, Hansard, procedure, and the primary public record of federal parliamentary work.

items.name: Australian Constitution
items.description: The legal foundation for Commonwealth power and federal structure.

items.name: Australian Electoral Commission
items.description: Election administration, formal results, and electoral integrity.

items.name: Department of the Prime Minister and Cabinet
items.description: Central executive guidance and Cabinet-facing administrative truth.

items.name: High Court of Australia
items.description: The highest authority on constitutional and legal boundaries.

tiers.title: Mature Interpreters
tiers.description: People and institutions that translate the field with judgment rather than noise.

items.name: Anne Twomey
items.description: Clear constitutional and institutional explanation anchored in Australian reality.

items.name: Lowy Institute
items.description: Useful for geopolitics, foreign policy, and statecraft with an Australian lens.

items.name: Parliamentary Library
items.description: Serious non-partisan background on bills, policy questions, and institutional context.

tiers.title: Practitioners
tiers.description: The working roles that make the system move.

items.name: APS policy officers
items.description: Translate political direction into options, briefs, and implementation.

items.name: Ministerial advisers
items.description: Carry political judgment, coordination, and risk management around ministers.

items.name: Committee and inquiry staff
items.description: Run scrutiny, evidence handling, and report-writing inside the accountability layer.

tiers.title: Institutions
tiers.description: The places where Australian political power is formalised, limited, and corrected.

items.name: Australian National Audit Office
items.description: The strongest starting point for understanding public administration failures and value-for-money concerns.

items.name: Commonwealth Ombudsman
items.description: A practical accountability body for administrative complaints and misconduct patterns.

items.name: National Anti-Corruption Commission
items.description: Part of the integrity architecture around serious corruption risks in the Commonwealth sphere.

items.name: Productivity Commission
items.description: A useful source for long-run policy and reform analysis beyond daily politics.

tiers.title: Media and Publications
tiers.description: Public writing that helps without pretending to be the field itself.

items.name: Inside Story
items.description: High-quality Australian politics and policy analysis.

items.name: The Conversation Australia
items.description: Academic-informed explanation that often adds useful context to public debate.

items.name: ABC Politics
items.description: Useful broad coverage with public-service context and reach.

tiers.title: Channels and Documentaries
tiers.description: Video and public-facing sources that help politics feel alive without dropping the institutional layer.

items.name: Parliament webcasts
items.description: Direct access to hearings, debates, and the procedural reality behind headlines.

items.name: ABC News Australia
items.description: Accessible Australian public affairs coverage and interviews.

items.name: Lowy Institute events
items.description: Useful when politics overlaps with geopolitics and statecraft.

tiers.title: Frontier Watchlist
tiers.description: The forces putting pressure on politics right now.

items.name: AI and political communication
items.description: Synthetic media, persuasion, and authenticity pressures inside democratic systems.

items.name: Institutional trust and democratic resilience
items.description: The health of democratic legitimacy is becoming a first-order political question.

items.name: Climate, industry, and security policy convergence
items.description: Domestic politics increasingly overlaps with long-run strategic and geopolitical choices.
